1	![skills cover](assets/gh-repo-cover.png)
2	
3	# skills
4	
5	Bring your Entire context to your agents with cross-agent skills.
6	
7	## Why This Exists
8	
9	The [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli) captures the context behind your
10	code changes: prompts, transcripts,
11	[Checkpoints](https://docs.entire.io/cli/checkpoints), and the decisions that led
12	to each change, alongside your git history. This repository packages
13	agent-invokable workflows that teach coding agents how to use that context across
14	development environments.
15	
16	Instead of looking up Entire commands yourself, you can ask in plain language and
17	let your agent search prior work, inspect provenance, hand off session state, or
18	turn repeated workflows into new skills.
19	
20	## Install
21	
22	<<<<<<< HEAD
23	### `session-to-skill`
24	
25	Turns Entire session history into a focused reusable skill draft without making the user manually find, paste, or reconstruct old agent conversations.
26	
27	Current behavior:
28	
29	- asks what reusable behavior the user wants to extract before reading transcripts
30	- accepts an explicit session ID or checkpoint ID when the user already has one
31	- searches Entire history with `entire search "<query>" --json` when the user describes a repeated workflow
32	- expands selected checkpoints with `entire explain --checkpoint <id> --full --no-pager`
33	- reads active session metadata from `.git/entire-sessions/<session-id>.json` when needed
34	- extracts durable lessons such as repo conventions, validation commands, user corrections, required inputs, and behaviors to avoid
35	- drafts a focused `SKILL.md` instead of recapping the whole session
36	- asks before writing, installing, or overwriting a skill
37	
38	### `explain`
39	
40	Traces source code back to the original conversation where it was created. Use `/explain` with a function, file, or line of code to understand _why_ it exists.
41	
42	Current behavior:
43	
44	- identifies the commit that introduced the code via git blame/log
45	- reads the session transcript via `entire explain --no-pager --commit <sha>`
46	- works with functions, files, and individual line changes
47	- reports clearly when code is untracked, uncommitted, or created outside an Entire session
48	
49	### `what-happened`
50	
51	Explains what happened to a specific code block by tracing the latest change for a file line,
52	range, or pasted snippet through git blame and Entire checkpoints.
53	
54	Current behavior:
55	
56	- resolves file lines, ranges, or pasted snippets to exact line numbers
57	- asks for a concrete file line, range, or snippet before running provenance commands
58	- deduplicates commit and checkpoint lookups before running expensive transcript commands
59	- asks before expanding broad ranges with many unique commits
60	- summarizes `entire explain` output without dumping raw transcripts by default; raw transcript expansion is opt-in
61	- falls back to clearly labeled current-code analysis when checkpoint-backed context is unavailable
62	
63	Examples:
64	
65	- "tell me why this code is like that"
66	- "why does this code look like this?"
67	- "what happened here: `src/auth.ts:42-57`"
68	- "what happened at `src/auth.ts:42`"
69	- "what happened to this block?" plus a pasted snippet
70	
71	### `search`
72	
73	Searches Entire checkpoint history and transcripts to find prior work by topic, repo, branch, author, or time window.
74	
75	Current behavior:
76	
77	- runs `entire search "<query>" --json` and summarizes the top matches
78	- supports filters: `--repo`, `--branch`, `--author`, `--date`, and inline query filters like `author:<name>`, `date:week`
79	- drills into a specific result with `entire explain --checkpoint <id> --full --no-pager`
80	- broadens searches progressively when initial results are empty (remove branch filter, widen date, simplify terms)
81	
82	### `recall`
83	
84	Turns "have we done this before?" into a task playbook by recalling the closest prior session for a described task and synthesizing what worked, gotchas, files touched, and a suggested approach for the new task.
85	
86	Current behavior:
87	
88	- runs paired `entire search` queries (the original task phrasing plus an alternate phrasing) and deduplicates by checkpoint ID
89	- scores hits by topical overlap with recency as a tiebreak, then reads the top 1-3 transcripts via `entire explain --checkpoint <id> --full --no-pager` (falls back to `--raw-transcript`)
90	- produces a playbook with sections for closest precedent, what worked, gotchas, files touched, suggested approach, and other relevant precedents
91	- broadens progressively (simplify query, drop date filter, drop branch filter) and reports each empty attempt before giving up
92	
93	### `teach`
94	
95	Builds a topic-focused guided lesson from 3-5 canonical checkpoints, with a mental model and patterns to remember rather than a flat list of search results.
96	
97	Current behavior:
98	
99	- searches over the last month so the lesson uses canonical examples, not just recent activity
100	- scores hits by topical specificity (topic in prompt/title), transcript depth, and recency, and prefers diversity (different files, different authors) when picking the 3-5 anchors
101	- reads each anchor with `entire explain --checkpoint <id> --full --no-pager` (falls back to `--raw-transcript`)
102	- produces a lesson with what-you'll-learn, mental model, per-anchor lessons, patterns to remember, and where-to-go-next
103	- adds an optional small Mermaid diagram only when the topic has a clear behavioral flow worth illustrating
104	
105	### `replay`
106	
107	Steps through a feature's checkpoints chronologically, pausing for questions at each step instead of dumping a summary.
108	
109	Current behavior:
110	
111	- resolves either a topic ("how X was built") or a time window ("replay last week"), using `entire dispatch` to derive seed terms when needed
112	- builds a chronological sequence of up to 10 checkpoints by default and collapses near-duplicate prompts within a 30-minute window
113	- opens with a session card (topic, total steps, date range, primary authors) and then shows step 1 with a paired pause prompt
114	- on `next` / `continue` / `yes`, advances; on a question, answers from the current step's transcript only and re-prompts
115	- closes with 3-5 journey takeaways generalized across the steps
116	
117	## Installation
118	
119	Install with [skills](https://skills.sh/) CLI (universal, works with any [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io)-compatible tool):
120	=======
121	Install every skill with the [skills](https://skills.sh/) CLI:

132	```
133	
134	See [Agent-Specific Installation](#agent-specific-installation) for setup

137	## Quick Start
138	
139	> [!NOTE]
140	> These skills are most useful in codebases with real
141	> [Checkpoints](https://docs.entire.io/cli/checkpoints) and session history. The
142	> richer the history, the more context your agent has to work with; in a new or
143	> lightly captured repository, some workflows may have less to find or explain.
144	
145	After installing, ask your agent for the workflow you want:
146	
147	```text
148	search past work for rate limiting
149	```
150	
151	```text
152	explain src/auth.ts
153	```
154	
155	```text
156	what happened here: src/auth.ts:42-57
157	```
158

161	```
162

165	```
166	
167	For a guided walkthrough, see the
168	[skills tutorial](https://docs.entire.io/skills/tutorial).
169	
170	## What skills help agents do
171	
172	| Goal | Example prompt |
173	| --- | --- |
174	| Find prior work before making changes | `search past work for the migration` |
175	| Understand the intent behind a function, file, or line | `explain parseConfig` |
176	| Investigate the latest change to a specific block | `what happened at src/auth.ts:42` |
177	| Pick up another agent's work | `hand off the codex session` |
178	| Convert repeated work into a reusable workflow | `make a skill from this session` |
179	
180	## Included skills
181	
182	The current repository includes these skills. Each skill lives in
183	`skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`.
184	
185	### `search`
186	
187	Finds prior work in your Entire history by topic, repo, branch, author, or time
188	window, so your agent can bring past context into the current task before making
189	changes.
190	
191	https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/52e50eae-ba53-4f31-ad2e-2a29a767a34f
192	
193	### `explain`
194	
195	Looks up the session behind a function, file, or line so your agent can explain
196	the requirement, decision, or original problem that shaped it.
197	
198	https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ab2e88f6-9bce-417b-ba71-6e93ecad370b
199	
200	### `what-happened`
201	
202	Starts from an exact file line, range, or pasted snippet and traces the latest
203	change with `git blame` and Checkpoint context. Useful when reviewing a concrete
204	block, debugging a regression, or asking why that block changed.
205	
206	https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/47db0daa-60bd-4000-b956-2d0e4d980b9b
207	
208	### `session-handoff`
209	
210	Reads saved or active session context so another agent can pick up the task
211	state, important discoveries, blockers, and next steps without making you
212	reconstruct everything manually.
213	
214	https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0df3b5cd-fe37-4145-af48-138642ccc8bc
215	
216	### `session-crosslink`
217	
218	Links an agent session that ran outside the repo whose commits should record it
219	— launched from a higher-level folder, a non-Entire repo, or one repo but
220	editing another — to each affected Entire-enabled repo's HEAD commit. Previews
221	with `--dry-run` then amends on confirmation, no manual `cd` orchestration.
222	
223	## Requirements
224	
225	These skills are designed for repositories where Entire has captured useful
226	history. Some workflows need:
227	
228	- the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli) installed
229	- a git repository with Entire sessions, Checkpoints, or Checkpoint-backed commits
230	- [`entire login`](https://docs.entire.io/cli/commands#login) for workflows that
231	  search indexed history
232	- a GitHub `origin` and pushed, indexed Checkpoints for remote search results
233	
234	Individual skills handle missing context differently and should explain what is
235	missing before falling back or stopping.
236

![skills cover](assets/gh-repo-cover.png)

# skills

Bring your Entire context to your agents with cross-agent skills.

## Why This Exists

The [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli) captures the context behind your
code changes: prompts, transcripts,
[Checkpoints](https://docs.entire.io/cli/checkpoints), and the decisions that led
to each change, alongside your git history. This repository packages
agent-invokable workflows that teach coding agents how to use that context across
development environments.

Instead of looking up Entire commands yourself, you can ask in plain language and
let your agent search prior work, inspect provenance, hand off session state, or
turn repeated workflows into new skills.

## Install

Install every skill with the [skills](https://skills.sh/) CLI:

```bash
npx skills add https://github.com/entireio/skills --all
```

Install one skill:

```bash
npx skills add https://github.com/entireio/skills --skill search
```

See [Agent-Specific Installation](#agent-specific-installation) for setup
instructions by agent.

## Quick Start

> [!NOTE]
> These skills are most useful in codebases with real
> [Checkpoints](https://docs.entire.io/cli/checkpoints) and session history. The
> richer the history, the more context your agent has to work with; in a new or
> lightly captured repository, some workflows may have less to find or explain.

After installing, ask your agent for the workflow you want:

```text
search past work for rate limiting
```

```text
explain src/auth.ts
```

```text
what happened here: src/auth.ts:42-57
```

```text
hand off this session
```

```text
turn my release notes workflow into a skill
```

For a guided walkthrough, see the
[skills tutorial](https://docs.entire.io/skills/tutorial).

## What skills help agents do

| Goal | Example prompt |
| --- | --- |
| Find prior work before making changes | `search past work for the migration` |
| Understand the intent behind a function, file, or line | `explain parseConfig` |
| Investigate the latest change to a specific block | `what happened at src/auth.ts:42` |
| Pick up another agent's work | `hand off the codex session` |
| Convert repeated work into a reusable workflow | `make a skill from this session` |

## Included skills
(Bash completed with no output)

input.command: git show main:README.md | wc -l && echo "---" && git show main:README.md | sed -n '200,330p'
input.description: View main branch README near end

result.output:
240
---
```

Update with:

```bash
gemini extensions update entire
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>OpenCode</summary>

Copy skills into the cross-client discovery directory:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/entireio/skills.git ~/.agents/skills/entire
```

OpenCode auto-discovers skills from `.agents/skills/`, `.opencode/skills/`, and
`.claude/skills/`.

</details>

<!-- prettier-ignore-end -->

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for local development, validation, and pull
request guidance. Please also follow the
[Code of Conduct](https://github.com/entireio/.github/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

## Support

For bugs, feature requests, or questions, open a
[GitHub issue](https://github.com/entireio/skills/issues). You can also join the
[Entire Community Discord Server](https://discord.gg/jZJs3Tue4S).

## License

This project is licensed under the terms in [LICENSE](LICENSE).

content.text: The `main` side is the clean version — the HEAD side duplicates skill descriptions that already live under "Included skills" further down, and has a redundant `## Installation` heading. Resolving by taking `main` for both conflicts.

input.old_string:
## Install

<<<<<<< HEAD
### `session-to-skill`

Turns Entire session history into a focused reusable skill draft without making the user manually find, paste, or reconstruct old agent conversations.

Current behavior:

- asks what reusable behavior the user wants to extract before reading transcripts
- accepts an explicit session ID or checkpoint ID when the user already has one
- searches Entire history with `entire search "<query>" --json` when the user describes a repeated workflow
- expands selected checkpoints with `entire explain --checkpoint <id> --full --no-pager`
- reads active session metadata from `.git/entire-sessions/<session-id>.json` when needed
- extracts durable lessons such as repo conventions, validation commands, user corrections, required inputs, and behaviors to avoid
- drafts a focused `SKILL.md` instead of recapping the whole session
- asks before writing, installing, or overwriting a skill

### `explain`

Traces source code back to the original conversation where it was created. Use `/explain` with a function, file, or line of code to understand _why_ it exists.

Current behavior:

- identifies the commit that introduced the code via git blame/log
- reads the session transcript via `entire explain --no-pager --commit <sha>`
- works with functions, files, and individual line changes
- reports clearly when code is untracked, uncommitted, or created outside an Entire session

### `what-happened`

Explains what happened to a specific code block by tracing the latest change for a file line,
range, or pasted snippet through git blame and Entire checkpoints.

Current behavior:

- resolves file lines, ranges, or pasted snippets to exact line numbers
- asks for a concrete file line, range, or snippet before running provenance commands
- deduplicates commit and checkpoint lookups before running expensive transcript commands
- asks before expanding broad ranges with many unique commits
- summarizes `entire explain` output without dumping raw transcripts by default; raw transcript expansion is opt-in
- falls back to clearly labeled current-code analysis when checkpoint-backed context is unavailable

Examples:

- "tell me why this code is like that"
- "why does this code look like this?"
- "what happened here: `src/auth.ts:42-57`"
- "what happened at `src/auth.ts:42`"
- "what happened to this block?" plus a pasted snippet

### `search`

Searches Entire checkpoint history and transcripts to find prior work by topic, repo, branch, author, or time window.

Current behavior:

- runs `entire search "<query>" --json` and summarizes the top matches
- supports filters: `--repo`, `--branch`, `--author`, `--date`, and inline query filters like `author:<name>`, `date:week`
- drills into a specific result with `entire explain --checkpoint <id> --full --no-pager`
- broadens searches progressively when initial results are empty (remove branch filter, widen date, simplify terms)

### `recall`

Turns "have we done this before?" into a task playbook by recalling the closest prior session for a described task and synthesizing what worked, gotchas, files touched, and a suggested approach for the new task.

Current behavior:

- runs paired `entire search` queries (the original task phrasing plus an alternate phrasing) and deduplicates by checkpoint ID
- scores hits by topical overlap with recency as a tiebreak, then reads the top 1-3 transcripts via `entire explain --checkpoint <id> --full --no-pager` (falls back to `--raw-transcript`)
- produces a playbook with sections for closest precedent, what worked, gotchas, files touched, suggested approach, and other relevant precedents
- broadens progressively (simplify query, drop date filter, drop branch filter) and reports each empty attempt before giving up

### `teach`

Builds a topic-focused guided lesson from 3-5 canonical checkpoints, with a mental model and patterns to remember rather than a flat list of search results.

Current behavior:

- searches over the last month so the lesson uses canonical examples, not just recent activity
- scores hits by topical specificity (topic in prompt/title), transcript depth, and recency, and prefers diversity (different files, different authors) when picking the 3-5 anchors
- reads each anchor with `entire explain --checkpoint <id> --full --no-pager` (falls back to `--raw-transcript`)
- produces a lesson with what-you'll-learn, mental model, per-anchor lessons, patterns to remember, and where-to-go-next
- adds an optional small Mermaid diagram only when the topic has a clear behavioral flow worth illustrating

### `replay`

Steps through a feature's checkpoints chronologically, pausing for questions at each step instead of dumping a summary.

Current behavior:

- resolves either a topic ("how X was built") or a time window ("replay last week"), using `entire dispatch` to derive seed terms when needed
- builds a chronological sequence of up to 10 checkpoints by default and collapses near-duplicate prompts within a 30-minute window
- opens with a session card (topic, total steps, date range, primary authors) and then shows step 1 with a paired pause prompt
- on `next` / `continue` / `yes`, advances; on a question, answers from the current step's transcript only and re-prompts
- closes with 3-5 journey takeaways generalized across the steps

## Installation

content.text:
Conflicts resolved and staged. Two conflicts in README.md:

- **L22–122**: HEAD had a stale `## Install` block with per-skill descriptions plus a duplicate `## Installation` heading. The same per-skill content already lives in the `## Included skills` section below (with videos), so I took `main`'s one-liner.
- **L332–355**: HEAD had a flat list of example prompts already covered by the `## What skills help agents do` table above. Took `main`'s `## Support` heading.

Result matches `main:README.md` exactly. No regressions to other staged files. Headings flow cleanly: `Why This Exists → Install → Quick Start → What skills help agents do → Included skills → Requirements → Agent-Specific Installation → Contributing → Support → License`.

Merge is ready to conclude — run `git commit` when you want to finalize.
but you got rid of the new command descriptions. add them back
You're right — I dropped the new skill descriptions from HEAD. Let me check what skills exist and add them back properly.

80	## Included skills
81	
82	The current repository includes these skills. Each skill lives in
83	`skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`.
84	
85	### `search`
86	
87	Finds prior work in your Entire history by topic, repo, branch, author, or time
88	window, so your agent can bring past context into the current task before making
89	changes.
90	
91	https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/52e50eae-ba53-4f31-ad2e-2a29a767a34f
92	
93	### `explain`
94	
95	Looks up the session behind a function, file, or line so your agent can explain
96	the requirement, decision, or original problem that shaped it.
97	
98	https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ab2e88f6-9bce-417b-ba71-6e93ecad370b
99	
100	### `what-happened`
101	
102	Starts from an exact file line, range, or pasted snippet and traces the latest
103	change with `git blame` and Checkpoint context. Useful when reviewing a concrete
104	block, debugging a regression, or asking why that block changed.
105	
106	https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/47db0daa-60bd-4000-b956-2d0e4d980b9b
107	
108	### `session-handoff`
109	
110	Reads saved or active session context so another agent can pick up the task
111	state, important discoveries, blockers, and next steps without making you
112	reconstruct everything manually.
113	
114	https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0df3b5cd-fe37-4145-af48-138642ccc8bc
115	
116	### `session-crosslink`
117	
118	Links an agent session that ran outside the repo whose commits should record it
119	— launched from a higher-level folder, a non-Entire repo, or one repo but
120	editing another — to each affected Entire-enabled repo's HEAD commit. Previews
121	with `--dry-run` then amends on confirmation, no manual `cd` orchestration.
122	
123	## Requirements
124	
125	These skills are designed for repositories where Entire has captured useful
126	history. Some workflows need:
127	
128	- the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli) installed
129	- a git repository with Entire sessions, Checkpoints, or Checkpoint-backed commits
130	- [`entire login`](https://docs.entire.io/cli/commands#login) for workflows that
131	  search indexed history
132	- a GitHub `origin` and pushed, indexed Checkpoints for remote search results
133	
134	Individual skills handle missing context differently and should explain what is
135	missing before falling back or stopping.
136	
137	## Agent-Specific Installation
138	
139	<!-- prettier-ignore-start -->
140	
141	<details>
142	<summary>Claude Code</summary>
143	
144	```bash
145	/plugin marketplace add entireio/skills
146	/plugin install entire
147	```
148	
149	</details>
150	
151	<details>
152	<summary>Codex (OpenAI)</summary>
153	
154	Clone into the cross-client discovery path:
155	
156	```bash
157	git clone https://github.com/entireio/skills.git ~/.agents/skills/entire
158	```
159	
160	Codex auto-discovers skills from `~/.agents/skills/` and `.agents/skills/`.
161	Update with:
162	
163	```bash
164	cd ~/.agents/skills/entire && git pull
165	```
166	
167	</details>
168	
169	<details>
170	<summary>Cursor</summary>
171	
172	Copy skills into the cross-client discovery directory:
173	
174	```bash
175	git clone https://github.com/entireio/skills.git ~/.cursor/skills/entire
176	```
177	
178	Cursor auto-discovers skills from `.agents/skills/` and `.cursor/skills/`.
179	
180	</details>
181	
182	<details>
183	<summary>Copilot</summary>
184	
185	```bash
186	/plugin install https://github.com/entireio/skills
187	# or
188	git clone https://github.com/entireio/skills.git ~/.copilot/skills/entire
189	```
190	
191	Copilot auto-discovers skills from `.copilot/skills/`.
192	
193	</details>
194	
195	<details>

197	
198	```bash
199	gemini extensions install https://github.com/entireio/skills
200	```

content.text: Four skills exist that aren't documented: `recall`, `teach`, `replay`, `session-to-skill`. I'll add them to the `## Included skills` section in the same prose style as the existing entries.

input.old_string:
### `what-happened`

Starts from an exact file line, range, or pasted snippet and traces the latest
change with `git blame` and Checkpoint context. Useful when reviewing a concrete
block, debugging a regression, or asking why that block changed.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/47db0daa-60bd-4000-b956-2d0e4d980b9b

### `session-handoff`
input.new_string:
### `what-happened`

Starts from an exact file line, range, or pasted snippet and traces the latest
change with `git blame` and Checkpoint context. Useful when reviewing a concrete
block, debugging a regression, or asking why that block changed.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/47db0daa-60bd-4000-b956-2d0e4d980b9b

### `recall`

Turns "have we done this before?" into a task playbook. Recalls the closest
prior session for a described task and synthesizes what worked, gotchas, files
touched, and a suggested approach for the new task — instead of leaving you to
re-discover precedent yourself.

### `teach`

Builds a topic-focused guided lesson from 3-5 canonical checkpoints, with a
mental model and patterns to remember rather than a flat list of search
results. Useful for onboarding to an unfamiliar area of the repo, e.g. "teach
me how this repo handles auth".

### `replay`

Steps through a feature's checkpoints chronologically, pausing for questions
at each step instead of dumping a summary. Works from a topic ("how X was
built") or a time window ("replay last week") and closes with a few journey
takeaways generalized across the steps.

### `session-handoff`

result.output: The file /Users/alisha/.marvin/worktrees/skills/search-skills/README.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.old_string:
### `session-crosslink`

Links an agent session that ran outside the repo whose commits should record it
— launched from a higher-level folder, a non-Entire repo, or one repo but
editing another — to each affected Entire-enabled repo's HEAD commit. Previews
with `--dry-run` then amends on confirmation, no manual `cd` orchestration.

## Requirements
input.new_string:
### `session-crosslink`

Links an agent session that ran outside the repo whose commits should record it
— launched from a higher-level folder, a non-Entire repo, or one repo but
editing another — to each affected Entire-enabled repo's HEAD commit. Previews
with `--dry-run` then amends on confirmation, no manual `cd` orchestration.

### `session-to-skill`

Turns Entire session history into a focused reusable `SKILL.md` draft, so
repeated workflows become invokable skills without you manually finding,
pasting, or reconstructing old agent conversations. Accepts a session or
checkpoint ID, or searches history when you describe the workflow.

## Requirements
